chemistry.mechanism_type: photoredox catalysis via single electron transfer

substrates.stoichiometry: 1 equiv thiol : 4 equiv CF3I (batch) or 1 equiv thiol : 1.1 equiv CF3I (flow)

photocatalyst.name: Ru(bpy)3Cl2
photocatalyst.role: generates CF3 radicals via single electron transfer

additives.name: triphenylphosphine/water
additives.role: in situ reduction of disulfides for aliphatic thiols

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: CF3 radical generated from CF3I via single electron transfer from Ru(I) species

chemistry.scope_summary: 32 examples (52-98% yield) including aromatic thiols with electron-withdrawing and electron-donating groups, heteroaromatic thiols (pyridine, benzoxazole, benzothiazole, benzimidazole, pyrimidine derivatives), and aliphatic thiols. Also demonstrated with other perfluoroalkyl halides like perfluorohexyl iodide, perfluorobutyl iodide, and ethyl 2-bromo-difluoroacetate

optimization_table.key_findings: Ru(bpy)3Cl2 with TEA additive gave best results with CF2SO2Cl as CF3 source

figures.caption: Scheme 1 Substrate scope for photocatalytic direct trifluoromethylation. [Section A] Aryl thiols (compounds 1b – 19b ), [Section B] heteroaryl thiols (compounds 20b – 26b ) and [Section C] aliphatic thiols ( 27b – 29b ). [a] Batch protocol: thiol (1 mmol), Ru(bpy) 3 Cl 2 (0.01 mmol), CF 3 I (4 mmol) in MeCN (5 mL). Then add TEA (1.1 mmol) and irradiate with a 24 W CFL household light bulb at r

figures.flow_path: Two inlet streams (aryl thiol with Ru(bpy)3Cl2 in acetonitrile, and TEA in acetonitrile) merge with CF3-I stream, pass through microflow reactor with LED irradiation, then through BPR to collection
figures.key_information: Photochemical trifluoromethylation setup with ruthenium photocatalyst, TEA as reductant, and CF3-I as trifluoromethyl source under blue LED irradiation
figures.caption: Fig. 1 Continuous-flow setup for the visible-light photocatalytic tri-fluoro-methylation of thiols. | Scheme 2 Synthetic applications of the photocatalytic tri-fluoromethylation protocol. (i) Use of disulfides as a substrate (eqn (4)); (ii) formation of sulfoxides via a one-pot photocatalytic tri-fluoromethylation/oxidation sequence (eqn (5a)), and exploitation of the orthogonal selectivity between photoredox catalysis and cross-coupling chemistry (eqn (5b)); (iii) photocatalytic per-fluoroalk | Fig. 2 Proposed mechanism for the photocatalytic tri-fluoromethylation of thiols.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Requires 60 minutes reaction time with 4 equivalents of CF3I
translation_logic.flow_advantage: 15-fold increase in production rate with 60-fold reduction in reaction time (1 min vs 60 min) and reduced CF3I usage (1.1 equiv vs 4 equiv)
translation_logic.what_changed: Reaction time reduced from 60 min to 1 min, CF3I equivalents reduced from 4 to 1.1, throughput increased 15-fold
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same catalyst (Ru(bpy)3Cl2), same yield (98%), same temperature (25°C), same solvent (MeCN)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Reduced CF3I inventory and shorter residence time in flow reactor

figure_descriptions: Flow reactor setup with blue LED irradiation
Mechanistic scheme showing photoredox catalytic cycle

conflicts: Pass3 shows number_of_inlets=2 but figure shows 3 streams merging
notes: Well-documented photocatalytic trifluoromethylation with clear batch-to-flow translation. Flow reactor scheme provides key missing quantitative details. Minor discrepancy in inlet count resolved using figure data.
